instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `with-lock-held` that takes a single-element binding `(lock-form)` and a body. The expansion uses UNWIND-PROTECT to acquire the lock (via `(acquire-lock lock-form)`), run the body, and release the lock (via `(release-lock lock-form)`) on any exit — normal, error, or non-local. Idiomatic CL resource-management pattern; the lock-form is re-evaluated in the cleanup branch.
